title: Harish Rana, India's first passive euthanasia case, dies after 13 years in vegetative state

Students must secure at least 33 per cent to qualify, while supplementary exams will be conducted for those who do not meet the criteria.

No SC status on conversion to religions other than Hinduism, Sikhism or Buddhism: Top court
description: The Supreme Court ruled that individuals converting from Hinduism, Sikhism, or Buddhism to other religions lose their Scheduled Caste status. The top court upheld an Andhra Pradesh High Court order, stating that professing a different faith, like Christianity, disqualifies one from SC benefits and protections under the SC/ST Act. This bar is absolute, regardless of birth or caste certificates.

This threat casts a shadow over the tournament, which the PCB insists will proceed as planned.

title: H-1B visa registrations dip 30-50%: List of cos emerging as ‘favourites’ in new system
description: H-1B visa registrations for FY27 have plummeted by 30-50%, driven by higher fees and a new lottery prioritizing wages. This shift benefits mid-tier IT firms and US Master's graduates, while large outsourcing companies face reduced dominance. The recalibration signals a move towards quality talent and increased costs for employers.

title: Ram Navami 2026: Date set for March 27; madhyahna muhurat 11:13 AM–1:41 PM
description: Ram Navami, celebrating the birth of Lord Shri Ram, the seventh incarnation of Lord Vishnu, will be observed on March 26, 2026. This prominent Hindu festival marks the end of Chaitra Navratri and is observed with prayers, recitations of Ramacharitmanas, and acts of charity, honoring Lord Rama's ideals of morality, truth, and justice.
